note: Latest Home Office Immigration system statistics release is year ending March 2026 (published 21 May 2026). Asylum appeals come from a separate publisher (HMCTS/Ministry of Justice tribunals statistics, January to March 2026). Reference periods differ between measures, so do not mix calendar-year, year-ending, and point-in-time figures.

metrics.metric_name: Asylum applications (people claiming asylum)
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people claim asylum in the UK?
metrics.notes: Counts people (main applicants and dependants). Down 12% on the year ending March 2025. Peak was 110,051 in the year ending September 2025.

metrics.metric_name: Asylum applications — main applicants (cases requiring a decision)
metrics.source_name: House of Commons Library, Asylum statistics (SN01403, 29 May 2026)
metrics.notes: Main-applicant total (excludes dependants); the count of asylum cases requiring Home Office consideration. 76,714 applications relating to 93,525 people, a 9% decrease on the previous 12 months.

metrics.metric_name: Asylum initial decision grant rate
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people are granted asylum in the UK?
metrics.notes: Proportion of initial decisions (main applicants) resulting in a grant of protection or other leave; excludes withdrawals and administrative outcomes. Down from 49% a year earlier; peak was 77% (year ending September 2022). The grant rate at final outcome, after appeals, is historically 17 to 29 percentage points higher, so this understates eventual protection rates.

metrics.metric_name: People granted protection or other leave at initial decision
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people are granted asylum in the UK?
metrics.notes: Includes main applicants and dependants. Up 5% on the previous year. Of 128,300 people who received an initial decision, 79,719 were refused (up 57%).

metrics.metric_name: Asylum backlog — people awaiting an initial decision
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people are in the UK asylum system?
metrics.notes: Relates to 35,744 cases. Down 55% on March 2025; lowest since September 2019; down 72% from the June 2023 peak of 175,457. A point-in-time stock figure.

metrics.metric_name: Asylum backlog — cases awaiting an initial decision
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people are in the UK asylum system?
metrics.notes: Cases are main applicants only. The 48,758 people awaiting an initial decision relate to these 35,744 cases.

metrics.metric_name: Total asylum cases in the casework system (work in progress)
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics (citing Migration Transparency Data ASY_03)
metrics.notes: Total work in progress: cases awaiting an initial decision plus those awaiting appeal or removal. NOT current — the latest full figure is June 2024 (a record high) because the wider breakdown was suspended pending a new caseworking system. Do not confuse with the current 'awaiting initial decision' figure.

metrics.metric_name: Small boat arrivals — calendar year 2025
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Irregular migration to the UK detailed dataset (illegal entry routes)
metrics.notes: Second-highest annual total on record (peak 45,774 in 2022); 13% higher than 2024. Finalised calendar-year total.

metrics.metric_name: Small boat arrivals — year ending March 2026
metrics.source_name: House of Commons Library, Statistics on small boat Channel crossings (citing Home Office IER_D03 / IER_02a)
metrics.notes: About a 3% increase year-on-year. Made up about 90% of all detected unauthorised arrivals (around 44,000 total). 95-99% of small boat arrivals go on to claim asylum.

metrics.metric_name: Small boat arrivals — 2026 year-to-date (to end May)
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Migrants detected crossing the English Channel in small boats (operational data)
metrics.notes: Provisional operational figure, approximate (around 9,000), reported as about 38% lower than the same period in 2025. Subject to revision; treat as indicative, not finalised statistics.

metrics.metric_name: Asylum appeals lodged (First-tier Tribunal)
metrics.source_name: HM Courts & Tribunals Service / Ministry of Justice, Tribunals statistics quarterly, January to March 2026
metrics.notes: A single quarter's inflow, not an annual figure (down from a possible peak of 17,822 the prior quarter).

metrics.metric_name: Asylum appeals backlog (First-tier Tribunal, outstanding cases)
metrics.source_name: HM Courts & Tribunals Service / Ministry of Justice, Tribunals statistics quarterly, January to March 2026
metrics.notes: A record high, up from about 50,976 a year earlier. Fewer than 7,000 appeals are decided per quarter, so the backlog keeps rising. The total immigration appeal caseload (all types) now exceeds 150,000.

metrics.metric_name: Asylum appeal success (allowed) rate
metrics.source_name: HM Courts & Tribunals Service / Ministry of Justice, Tribunals statistics quarterly, January to March 2026
metrics.notes: About 40% of asylum appeals allowed. Mean age of an asylum appeal at conclusion: 67 weeks.

metrics.metric_name: People in receipt of asylum support (total)
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people are in the UK asylum system?
metrics.notes: In receipt of accommodation and/or subsistence support. Down 9% on March 2025; 21% below the September 2023 peak of 123,758. Excludes unaccompanied asylum-seeking children (supported by local authorities).

metrics.metric_name: People in asylum hotel accommodation
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people are in the UK asylum system?
metrics.notes: 21% of the supported population. Down 35% on March 2025 and 63% below the September 2023 peak of 56,018. A further 75% were in other accommodation and 4% on subsistence-only support.

metrics.metric_name: Largest nationality claiming asylum — Pakistan (share)
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — How many people claim asylum in the UK?
metrics.notes: Pakistan was the single largest nationality (about 10%). Top six (Pakistan, Eritrea, Iran, Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Sudan) together about 46% of claimants. Notable changes: Somali claims up 151%, Eritrean up 46%, Sudanese up 20%; Syrian down 72% (after the fall of the Assad regime in December 2024), Vietnamese down 56%.

metrics.metric_name: Ukraine schemes — cumulative out-of-country visas granted
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — safe and legal (humanitarian) routes
metrics.notes: Visas granted, not arrivals (some unused). 14,725 out-of-country grants in the latest year. About 105,900 people (45%) who arrived had left the UK by end March 2026. A further 140,191 in-country extensions (mostly Ukraine Permission Extension scheme) were granted in the latest year.

metrics.metric_name: Hong Kong BN(O) visas — cumulative grants since January 2021
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — safe and legal (humanitarian) routes
metrics.notes: Out-of-country BN(O) visa grants since the route opened on 31 January 2021. 7,214 grants in the latest year; grants have fallen every year since the year ending March 2022 peak of 86,439.

metrics.metric_name: Afghan Resettlement Programme — cumulative arrivals
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — safe and legal (humanitarian) routes
metrics.notes: Breakdown: ARAP 20,028; ACRS 14,091; Afghanistan Response Route 4,468. In July 2025 the government confirmed the schemes were closing to new applicants; existing applications are still being processed.

metrics.metric_name: Total people resettled (all schemes), latest year
metrics.source_name: Home Office, Immigration system statistics, year ending March 2026 — safe and legal (humanitarian) routes
metrics.notes: Down 37% on the previous year. 90% via the Afghan Resettlement Programme; 10% via the UK Resettlement Scheme and Mandate Scheme. Most common nationality Afghan (90%).
